title: TEV Protease
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"2m8lb","text":"TEV Protease is a highly specific cysteine protease that recognizes the amino-acid sequence Glu-Asn-Leu-Tyr-Phe-Gln-(Gly/Ser) and cleaves between the Gln and Gly/Ser residues.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"b2mvn","text":"Removal of affinity purification tags such as maltose-binding protein (MBP) or poly-histidine from fusion proteins","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"fqnhj","text":"Optimal activity and stability for up to 24 months","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"1mrtg","text":"Active in a wide range of buffers; optimal activity between pH 6.0 and 9.0.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4ub4f","text":"High substrate specificity with no non-specific proteolysis","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"6fdls","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ff2br","text":"TEV Protease (NEB# P8112) is recommended for cleavage of a His-tag following purification with NEBExpress® Ni-NTA Magnetic Beads, Ni Spin Columns or Ni Resin. First, the expression vector must be designed to contain a TEV site between the His-tag and the protein. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sup","offset":105,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":14,"length":10}],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"3072ACA8827611EABF5D0242AC110002","url":"https://www.neb.com/products/p8112-tev-protease"}}}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"6ugjq","text":"Notes:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"80qt6","text":"If the fusion protein sample contains \u003e2 M urea, \u003e0.5 M Guanidine hydrochloride, \u003e50 mM imidazole, pH values below 6 or above 9, or cysteine protease inhibitors then it will be necessary to dialyze the fusion protein into TEV protease reaction buffer before TEV Protease cleavage.","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"3ulv8","text":"TEV protease is inhibited by reaction buffers containing \u003e40% Glycerol.","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"dkqmc","text":"Inhibition occurs in the presence of ≥ 5 mM Zn2+, ≥ 1 mM Cu2+and ≥ 10 mM Co2+.","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sup","offset":46,"length":2},{"style":"sup","offset":59,"length":2},{"style":"sup","offset":75,"length":2}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"f60h0","text":"Compatible with 10mM MgSO4, MnCl2and CaCl2and up to 100mM EDTA.","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sub","offset":25,"length":1},{"style":"sub","offset":32,"length":1},{"style":"sub","offset":41,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"1k78c","text":"Compatible with the following protease inhibitors: aprotinin, benzamidine, leupeptin, pepstatin, PMSF.","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"l5eb","text":"Optimal activity achieved in ≤0.2M NaCl; however, the enzyme retains some activity in up to 2M NaCl.","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"3tuf8","text":"Some substrates may require extended incubation periods (up to three days at either 4°C or 30°C) to achieve complete cleavage. The addition of more TEV Protease after 24 hours may also help achieve complete cleavage of some substrates.","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"5um6u","text":"Optimal incubation times and enzyme concentrations must be determined empirically for a particular substrate. Reactions may be scaled-up linearly to accommodate larger sample amounts and reaction volumes. Typical reaction conditions are as follows:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"7qpkl","text":"Please refer to the Safety Data Sheets (SDS) for health and environmental hazards. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Dialyze the protein against 20 millimolar (mM), Tris-HCl ,, pH 7.5 ..
Combine 15 µg, of protein and H20 (if necessary) to make a 45 µL total reaction volume.
Add 5 µL, TEV Protease Reaction Buffer (10X) to make a 50 µL total reaction volume.
Incubate at 30 °C for 01:00:00 or at 4 °C Overnight .
creator: New England Biolabs Tech Support
